lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Irene
lines.text: Hello, my name is Irene. I'm a chemist.

lines.speaker: Irene
lines.text: We're not entirely sure. I'm here to study it to find out just that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you tried....

lines.speaker: Irene
lines.text: Well, someone drank it... that didn't turn out so well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened? Who was it? Are they ok?

lines.speaker: Irene
lines.text: Well, she's still alive at the moment. It was my mum, Marie. She's a better chemist than me, but she likes to do things practically. This time it didn't work out so well for her. Lucky I was on hand to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hope she recovers soon.

lines.speaker: Irene
lines.text: Me too.

lines.speaker: Irene
lines.text: Ahh yes. Taking samples. We've tried all the methods we can think of, but it seems to, well, change somehow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What changes?

lines.speaker: Irene
lines.text: The substance. It's almost like taking a finger off a human and assuming it will stay alive I guess. The sample sort of... goes inert once we take the sample from the main body of the pool.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How awkward.

lines.speaker: Irene
lines.text: Yes, we've had a bit of progress there. We've tried adding in a lot of things with varying results. The accident was the best though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: Irene
lines.text: Well, I slipped and dropped a fossilised leg bone from something in the pool. It... well it kind of ate it away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Was there anything left?

lines.speaker: Irene
lines.text: Oh yes, it was quite interesting. It's as if it restored some of the biological content of the bone and stripped all the mineralisation of fosilisation away. The bone was, rather floppy though. Like when you remove all the

lines.speaker: Irene
lines.text: calcium and phosphate from it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you tried putting calcium in the pool?

lines.speaker: Irene
lines.text: Calcium and phosphate are highly reactive - you'd never find just those in any quantity! You could maybe find some within minerals around this island that could work.
